title: Mecodium flabellatum
taxonomy: Mecodium flabellatum is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Mecodium, which is a group of epiphytic ferns native to Australia and the Pacific Islands. The species Mecodium flabellatum is specifically classified as Mecodium flabellatum, with the common name being Fluted fern. Its classification also includes the kingdom Plantae, phylum Pteridophyta, and class Polypodiopsida. As of my last knowledge update, the classification for Mecodium flabellatum remains unchanged.

intro: Mecodium flabellatum, also known as the fan fern or bird's nest fern, is a species of epiphytic fern that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. The health benefits of Mecodium flabellatum include its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various health conditions such as fever, sore throats, and respiratory infections.
